questions.question: What is Big O notation?
options: B) Mathematical notation describing algorithm's worst-case time/space complexity as input grows
questions.correctAnswer: B) Mathematical notation describing algorithm's worst-case time/space complexity as input grows

questions.question: What is the time complexity of binary search?
questions.correctAnswer: C) O(log n)

questions.question: What is the two-pointer technique?
options: B) Two indices moving through array to solve problems in O(n) instead of O(n²)
questions.correctAnswer: B) Two indices moving through array to solve problems in O(n) instead of O(n²)

questions.question: What is the sliding window pattern?
options: B) Maintain contiguous subarray window, slide it to avoid recalculating — O(n) instead of O(n²)
questions.correctAnswer: B) Maintain contiguous subarray window, slide it to avoid recalculating — O(n) instead of O(n²)

questions.question: What is Floyd's cycle detection algorithm?
options: B) Slow pointer moves 1 step, fast moves 2 — they meet if cycle exists in linked list
questions.correctAnswer: B) Slow pointer moves 1 step, fast moves 2 — they meet if cycle exists in linked list

questions.question: What is the advantage of linked list over array?
options: B) O(1) insertion/deletion at known node — no shifting required
questions.correctAnswer: B) O(1) insertion/deletion at known node — no shifting required

questions.question: What is amortized O(1) for dynamic array append?
options: B) Most appends are O(1) but occasional resize is O(n) — averages to O(1) over many operations
questions.correctAnswer: B) Most appends are O(1) but occasional resize is O(n) — averages to O(1) over many operations

questions.question: What is a monotonic stack?
options: B) Stack maintaining elements in monotonically increasing or decreasing order — used for next greater/smaller element
questions.correctAnswer: B) Stack maintaining elements in monotonically increasing or decreasing order — used for next greater/smaller element

questions.question: What is prefix sum used for?
options: B) Precomputing cumulative sums enabling O(1) range sum queries after O(n) preprocessing
questions.correctAnswer: B) Precomputing cumulative sums enabling O(1) range sum queries after O(n) preprocessing

questions.question: What is a deque?
options: B) Double-ended queue allowing O(1) insert/delete from both front and back
questions.correctAnswer: B) Double-ended queue allowing O(1) insert/delete from both front and back

questions.question: What is the time complexity of accessing nth element in linked list?
options: B) O(n) — must traverse from head
questions.correctAnswer: B) O(n) — must traverse from head

questions.question: How do you find middle of linked list in one pass?
options: B) Slow pointer (1 step) and fast pointer (2 steps) — when fast reaches end, slow is at middle
questions.correctAnswer: B) Slow pointer (1 step) and fast pointer (2 steps) — when fast reaches end, slow is at middle

questions.question: What is space complexity O(1) mean?
options: B) Constant extra memory regardless of input size — in-place algorithm
questions.correctAnswer: B) Constant extra memory regardless of input size — in-place algorithm

questions.question: What is a hash collision?
options: B) Two different keys producing same hash value — resolved by chaining or open addressing
questions.correctAnswer: B) Two different keys producing same hash value — resolved by chaining or open addressing

questions.question: What is the average time complexity of HashMap lookup?
options: B) O(1) average — O(n) worst case with many collisions
questions.correctAnswer: B) O(1) average — O(n) worst case with many collisions

questions.question: What is a min-heap property?
options: B) Parent node is always smaller than or equal to its children — root is minimum element
questions.correctAnswer: B) Parent node is always smaller than or equal to its children — root is minimum element

questions.question: What is the time complexity of heapify?
options: B) O(n) — bottom-up heapify is O(n) though inserting n elements one-by-one is O(n log n)
questions.correctAnswer: B) O(n) — bottom-up heapify is O(n) though inserting n elements one-by-one is O(n log n)

questions.question: What is a circular linked list?
options: B) Linked list where last node points back to head — useful for round-robin scheduling
questions.correctAnswer: B) Linked list where last node points back to head — useful for round-robin scheduling

questions.question: What is the difference between stack and queue?
options: B) Stack is LIFO (Last In First Out); Queue is FIFO (First In First Out)
questions.correctAnswer: B) Stack is LIFO (Last In First Out); Queue is FIFO (First In First Out)

questions.question: What is dynamic programming?
options: B) Optimization technique breaking problem into overlapping subproblems, storing results (memoization/tabulation)
questions.correctAnswer: B) Optimization technique breaking problem into overlapping subproblems, storing results (memoization/tabulation)

questions.question: What is a Binary Search Tree (BST) property?
options: B) Left subtree nodes < root < right subtree nodes — enables O(log n) search in balanced BST
questions.correctAnswer: B) Left subtree nodes < root < right subtree nodes — enables O(log n) search in balanced BST

questions.question: What is inorder traversal of BST?
options: B) Left → Root → Right — produces sorted ascending order for BST
questions.correctAnswer: B) Left → Root → Right — produces sorted ascending order for BST

questions.question: What is BFS vs DFS?
options: B) BFS uses queue, explores level by level; DFS uses stack/recursion, explores depth-first
D) BFS is for trees only
questions.correctAnswer: B) BFS uses queue, explores level by level; DFS uses stack/recursion, explores depth-first

questions.question: What is a balanced binary tree?
options: A) Tree with equal nodes on both sides
B) Height difference between left and right subtrees of any node is at most 1
questions.correctAnswer: B) Height difference between left and right subtrees of any node is at most 1

questions.question: What is topological sort?
options: B) Linear ordering of vertices in DAG where for every edge u→v, u appears before v
questions.correctAnswer: B) Linear ordering of vertices in DAG where for every edge u→v, u appears before v

questions.question: What is Dijkstra's algorithm used for?
options: B) Finding shortest path from source to all vertices in weighted graph with non-negative weights
questions.correctAnswer: B) Finding shortest path from source to all vertices in weighted graph with non-negative weights

questions.question: What is a trie data structure?
options: B) Tree-like structure storing strings — each node is a character, used for prefix search in O(L)
questions.correctAnswer: B) Tree-like structure storing strings — each node is a character, used for prefix search in O(L)

questions.question: What is a segment tree?
options: B) Tree enabling range queries (min/max/sum) and updates in O(log n) time
questions.correctAnswer: B) Tree enabling range queries (min/max/sum) and updates in O(log n) time

questions.question: What is Union-Find (Disjoint Set)?
options: B) Data structure tracking elements in disjoint sets — supports union and find in near O(1) with path compression
questions.correctAnswer: B) Data structure tracking elements in disjoint sets — supports union and find in near O(1) with path compression

questions.question: What is memoization vs tabulation in DP?
options: B) Memoization is top-down recursive with cache; tabulation is bottom-up iterative filling table
questions.correctAnswer: B) Memoization is top-down recursive with cache; tabulation is bottom-up iterative filling table

questions.question: Which data structure uses LIFO?
questions.correctAnswer: B) Stack

questions.question: Which data structure is used in BFS?
questions.correctAnswer: B) Queue

questions.question: Which traversal is DFS?
questions.correctAnswer: B) Preorder
